There's a hatch leading further down. Looks used recently.
GET DOWN! GET DOWN! ECHO SQUAD, CONTAIN THE TARGET!
Bakay has been proven to be a traitor to The Office. He is a liability as long as he is alive. Terminate him.
Oleg Bakay! We know you're here. There is no way out of this shelter. Throw your weapons into the middle of the room, and come out from your position with your hands on your head.

Target down. <d target="data_bayte_dead">Oleg Bakay is dead.</d>

Very well. Mission accomplished. Make sure the area is secure, then disengage.
Bakay did not betray our secrets. He is too valuable and must be retrieved alive. Is that understood?
Roger. Get down and put your palms flat on the ground in front of you.
I SAID GET DOWN! Oleg Bakay, surrender and lie down. NOW!
Target cooperative. We got him. <d target="data_bakay_custody">Oleg Bakay is in custody.</d>
This is the school's library, still intact. Someone put a lot of effort into turning this into some sort of autonomous bomb shelter.
Absolutely. Move in. And keep me posted at all times. Describe to me everything you see.
And they succeeded. Even the power is still on! From what I can tell, this thing even has working surveillance cams on the walls.
Sorry, Command, I don’t quite follow.
The location. Tell me what you SEE.
Affirmative. But... the damage is catastrophic. Are you sure the target is around?
There are some adjacent rooms that seem rather intact. Echo 2, Echo 4, clear 'em.
Listen. I NEED you to find the target person. He IS around.
Hate to say it, but we may be out of lu--
Peculiar. Maybe Bakay is able to use them. Proceed with caution.
It's the target. It has to be.
Yeah... yeah… so far, so good.
I called to tell you I'll be home late today. I'm sorry.
Again? They don't pay you enough for all the time you give them.
It's not about payment and you know that. These people rely on me. They need me.
Yeah? Well I need <d target="data_raban_marriedkaren">my wife</d> at home with me some of the time. Am I asking for too much?
Raban... Not so long ago you were among them. You have been in their position!

You want me to cherry-pick who deserves my assistance and who doesn't? All people get the same treatment, the same attention. That’s my mandate.
One more reason to avoid her.
The hallway has been severely damaged. You can see the sky right through the multiple stories. Some parts are completely caved in. Proceed with caution, Echo squad.
It's... It's okay. So, are you good with me getting home late tonight then?
I will be waiting for you.

Well, I can hardly go anywhere else.
I thought... I thought you loved that about me?
You shouldn't say things like that. Anyway, I'll talk to you later.
The damage definitely originated some time ago. There's vegetation spreading all over the place.
Oh, hey Raban. I... didn't expect you to call. I'm getting ready for work.
Uhh, right now? Is it important?
What do you want me to write about?
There has been a second assault in Bonton. At Stelligan University.
Another one? Oh, wow, they really have got a situation here, don't they?
We need to cover it. Draw attention to how the government is failing its people.
Rooms are clear. Lots of... bags lying around. There are... still some geometrical drawings on the chalkboard. The place was clearly left in a hurry.
I'm not quite sure if I'll be able to pull it off at work. I feel like they're watching me already.
Do you stand with me, brother? Or has The Nation's corruption and wealth finally swayed your sympathies?
Patience, brother. It won't be long now. Take care of the article.
Okay. I will do my best to get it done for you. Gimme… gimme about an hour for the draft. I'll put it in the backend.
Good. I'll be waiting. Until later.
Raban... I stand with you. I’ve told you this.
Then I have faith in you, brother.
I have no idea what's going on with the donations. I swear they were working, and I haven't changed anything in the backend.
We have arrived at the designated location.
Mhm. So, you lied in the Aptitude Test and willingly admitted to it. You are either downright naive, or...
... you are very loyal towards us. You might be exactly whom I need.
Well. Right after the Safety Bill came into effect. Bad timing from their point of view, I guess.
Luckily Bakay is using one of our registered phone devices to communicate with us. It seems to be online right now.
You can access it using the Insider tool, which I will unlock for you now.
He tried to warn us that Vhart had compromised him? Why did the messages never reach us?
He tried to warn us that Vhart had compromised his cover? How could Vhart have done that? How did he know?
There's no way Raban Vhart could have obtained official documents and put them on Percoleaks. There must be someone else behind it.
Keep looking for an answer. There must be something we haven't found out about this mess yet.
As you see, Oleg Bakay is an important figure in the Parges' army, our neighboring country. We are very concerned about his fate.
Saving others while risking his own life. Now I see why his followers treat him almost like a religious figure.
Well, agent, I think it has become clear already that Raban Vhart is only a shadow of his former self. His actions today are what matters.
One more thing before I let you roam freely. Remember that we are bound to the division of powers defined by the Ethical Codex.
Damn it! I knew he must have been revealed! ... This... changes everything.

If Bakay's secret is out there, then that's how Vhart came across the info. It might have played a role in the call.
Agent, I hate to say it, but with this new perspective on the situation, we have to put Bakay's loyalty into question.
If you find any hints at possible disloyalty of our agent towards us, you need to let me know immediately.
If Bakay's secret is out there, then that's how Vhart came across the info. But this alone doesn't appear to be enough of a motive.
The FTP is a ruthless bunch, that's for sure.
And yet he sympathizes with the FTP? How does that make any sense?
Yes. Bakay is our informant. Placed inside the Pargesian army for years - now vanished into thin air. You surely grasp the danger this poses should it ever come out.
We need to understand what happened, and then possibly retrieve him. Or... contain the damage.
Doubt anything you read. Especially when it comes to The People's Voice. Don't give me wrong info to work with. I'm counting on you.
To get to the bottom of this, we will have to investigate the person who called him last: Raban Vhart.
You already found a trace to him before on the Beholder. I will turn Vhart into a target person now. Time to pick up from there.
The Beholder mentioned his threat to Oleg Bakay, remember? Time to pick the trace up from there.
They had some kind of deal going on at Vhart's school?
This strongly backs the theory of Bakay being involved in the attack on the school.
A deal at a school? What about?
Check the transcript for newly found Datachunks.
Good. This profile is now primed on Oleg Bakay. Now other Datachunks related to this person might appear.
Not exactly the most tender smile. But that's not what we're here for after all.
Orwell was designed in such way that not one person alone can look at the documents and interpret the data to make a judgment.
Raban Vhart. That's the name that Oleg Bakay mentioned during the call.
Let's focus on Bakay for the moment. We will get back to Vhart in a second.
As you see, a new profile has been added. But since Vhart isn't a target person, Orwell will not search for related documents.
That's an odd thing to say, isn't it? We're taking that as a death threat, as did The National Beholder.
Now, whenever you upload a Datachunk, Orwell will search for online documents with related content and add them to your bookmarks.
A bloody mess, that Civil War. Parges has suffered a great deal from it. So did The Nation...
The People's Voice. We have heard of them. This preposterous "news" site has people rallying for protests in Parges for years.
Judging by that call, Raban Vhart and Oleg Bakay seemed well acquainted, even if it isn't the most... friendly relationship, apparently.
Then, in order to understand whether Vhart is behind Bakay's disappearance, we need to find out what connects the two in the first place.
Your task here is two-fold, agent. First and foremost, we are to locate Oleg Bakay.
You're now leaving charted territory as far as I am concerned, and I have to rely on the data you upload to make my calls.
He isn't happy about Oleg's disappearance? How come I don't buy it?
Why, yes, of course! The death threat uttered in the phone call! That's why!
An autonomous shelter at a school? Seems a bit overprotective if you ask me. But then again...
Considering he thought about getting the army for protection, this pales in comparison.
Perfect. With this number we can track Raban Vhart's calls to and from his mobile phone within the Listener tool.
Well, that might explain his preachy attitude.
Of course we do inspect and observe the habits of our agents. What did you expect?
Oh, please. Please. I remember asking you explicitly not to give me any misinformation to work with.
How does this statement look to you? Does it look legitimate? Perhaps picking you as agent wasn't the best choice after all.
Ahh. I see. The two know each other back from where they lived in Parges. Well done, agent.
Try disabling Datachunks that seem illegitimate or unimportant to you in the Datachunk information window.
Prava. Oleg Bakay is in his former hometown. Peculiar. Why is he hiding out there? Or rather... from whom? Raban Vhart?
Excellent work. Since you have helped clarify the circumstances of his disappearance we will now be able to look for him.
Excellent work. We will retrieve Oleg Bakay once we have a clear picture as to why he has gone missing in the first place, and what that call meant.
Wait. Army protection for a school? Seriously? Things must have been bad. Or someone must have been really worried.
Is that so? Mhm. Doesn't exactly account for his loyalty to us.
Wait a second. If Bakay's daughter was present, then we can be pretty certain that he wasn't involved in the attack.
With Vhart as her caretaker, he must have been aware. Meaning, there must be more behind Vhart's threat.
If Bakay's daughter was present, then surely he was not involved in the attack.
A military bombardment. Mhm. The Pargesian army. This indicates that Bakay might indeed be involved in the attack.
I am the one who decides what to do with said information, and to take action. The adviser.
"President Puppet." I've seen the memes before. The Nation would have zero factual benefit from "control" over Parges.
Mhm. I guess we're treading into territory where facts aren't given any weight.
But... he sympathizes with them despite they are responsible for the attack on his school?
Now that's a classic one - demonize the free press. Straight from the Dictator's Guide to Supreme Rule.
Hah. We got a little hubris going, haven't we? No such thing will happen.
Kassart and Blaine working together is the best thing that could have happened to Parges. Why destroy it?
Raban Vhart married his assigned social worker? Do you find this as odd as I do?
I hope you don't expect me to feel pity for Vhart. I don't.
Supported by a National donation network? Mhm.
You on the other hand are the one doing the searching for data, as well as deciding what to upload, as you will often find misleading and false information.
If we were to prove that Raban Vhart is using the donations for something "harmful" we could pull the plug on him.
I cannot help but wonder how they would react if we tipped them towards Vhart's statement about the most recent assault.
What? That's quite the coincidence there, isn't it? Oleg Bakay... what have you done?
This could very well mean Bakay is directly involved in the attack on Vhart's school.
Raban Vhart mixes truth and lies adeptly to trick people. Take whatever he publishes with a grain of salt, as there are typically few truths.
Be very, very careful when dealing with him, agent. I have to rely solely on you.
This... genuinely surprises me. How did that happen?
Looks like you are getting your chance to investigate a medical profile, even if it's not your own like in the Aptitude Test.
While, yes, we are in fact worried about our relations with Parges because of Bakay, you will see that the whole truth is more... complex.
Mhm. Good. That might account for his loyalty towards us.
What? He talked to Kassart directly and didn't inform us?
Damn it. I'll have to assume this shows disloyalty towards us. Otherwise he would have reported that in.
What the hell is that supposed to mean? Hiding? You mean voluntarily?
There was no plan which included "going in hiding" anywhere!
Invasion? Tsk. Our efforts in Parges were justified operations to restore and keep the peace.
And we succeeded, if I might add that.
If he really believes that, this would be a strong motive. Good.
We can't work solely on what Raban Vhart "believes", though. We need to clarify whether there are any more hints of Bakay being involved in this.
This pretty much proves our suspicions about Raban Vhart wanting revenge for his school. This is all we need.
As you're not a simple investigator, you have access to an Orwell build designed to meet the specific needs of The Office.
This makes no sense at all. We already know Vhart probably had nothing to do with the attack.
Vhart is bluffing. Don't fall for it. There must be another reason for what's going on.
The number for Bakay's protected Office phone is out there?! This cannot be happening!
This is how Raban Vhart reached him, and who knows how many other people have his info now. This is a complete disaster!
What? Still? I doubt that somehow.
Oh yes. Oleg Bakay is a paragon of honor.
For all we know, this guy has been trying to cook up a large-scale stir for years. I don't see it happening quite yet.
Yes, he has succeeded to some extends with the ongoing riots. But we can keep the situation under control.
... you are very loyal towards us. You might be exactly whom I need.
We can UTILIZE the data stored in the Profiler in the left, to circumvent certain restrictions that would otherwise bar us from making progress.
Make good use of this ability within our database, and the data stored in Bakay's profile so far, to solve my little mystery.
You have gathered all we need. I will get an intervention team ready from a local base near Prava to go and look for Bakay.
Raban Vhart was a school principal back in Prava, Parges. He was also the teacher of Oleg Bakay's daughter, who went to fight in the Pargesian army.
The school was attacked by FTP rebels. Vhart managed to save the students by leading them to the shelter he built, but he lost his ability to walk.
The school was bombarded by military fire. Vhart managed to save the students by leading them to the shelter he built, but he lost his ability to walk.
The school was destroyed by fire of unknown origin. Vhart managed to save the students by leading them to the shelter he built, but he lost his ability to walk.
From the info we have collected it's unlikely Bakay had anything to do with the attack.
Based on the info we have collected, it's possible Bakay was involved in the attack somehow. It's also possible that Vhart learned about it and plotted revenge.
Vhart blames him regardless. Probably because he found out Bakay is a National agent through a leak.
... you are willing to shift the truth to your liking. You might be exactly whom I need.
We have no indication that Oleg Bakay was disloyal to us, so we have to assume he's innocent in this leak.
A leak which Oleg Bakay presumably caused himself. What a disloyal fool.
This is it, the theory about the situation we're facing. We will make sure to deal with it accordingly.
They're on their way. Now let me try and make sense of everything we've gathered. I need to decide how we should approach this:
If we had been aware of his involvement in this act of aggression towards a school... we would never have recruited him.
The intervention team is in place. I'm switching over to command, and I'll let you listen in via the Listener tool.
Now Bakay is hiding out somewhere in Prava, for whatever reason.
Mhm. Of course. Bakay has locked his phone with our own encryption tool. I am afraid not even Orwell can break that.
You will have to do this the old-fashioned way - by finding and applying Bakay's key. See if you can find a hint to discover his password.
My name is Ampleford. I am a part of a special division within the government, The Office. We get called in for the... delicate matters.
You may have to dig a little deeper to uncover it though, so it may be advisable to check other resources first.
"Janus." How fitting. He still goes by this call sign.
I'm granting you access to our database, which you should have a look at. You'll understand it better after you see the files.
Since you are paired with an investigator, I'll assume you're familiar with the procedure and spare you most technicalities.
So, I see that you are not paired with an investigator... I guess I better walk you through some of the basics then.
Within Orwell you take the role of the "investigator." Mind you, you're not a regular investigator, though, but rather an agent on special duty.
For starters, upload Oleg Bakay's name from the transcript to his file by dragging it into the Profiler on the left.
Your main task is to search online documents and extract relevant pieces of data, called "Datachunks".
Those Datachunks appear in a multitude of documents, but will always relate to our investigative target in some way. In our case that target is Bakay.
The National Beholder is the most respectable news site in The Nation. You may browse their headlines freely to get informed.
A man called Oleg Bakay disappeared a couple of days ago. The last we heard of him was the call I just played to you.
The recording of the call you heard ended up in their newsroom. Why? That's a piece of the puzzle we're missing right now.
However, this article is a good starting point for our investigation on Oleg Bakay. To proceed, please adapt his picture and profession.
You did it. Well done. I'll leave you to your well deserved info for a moment. To truly understand why this case is so precarious.
Orwell reports you have removed the encryption from Bakay's device. Open sesame.
Good work, agent. That will aid us greatly.
I need you to investigate and upload anything that you think is relevant, and accurate, about the background of these two. Discard anything else.
From what you gather, I will put together a theory about what happened, and what we're going to do about it.
With the basic connection between the two clarified, our in-depth research of the lead up to this phone call can now begin.
At the very least we need a solid motive for Raban Vhart making this threat to Oleg Bakay, and an explanation as to how Vhart could contact him on his protected phone.
If you need to keep track of your objectives, click on the Orwell eye in the upper left corner of the screen or press ESC.
I have added a transcript of that dialogue to your Reader tool. You can read it again, or listen to the recording. When you're done, open the "Unknown person" profile on the left.
Orwell notifies me you just encountered a conflicted Datachunk. I have the feeling this will be a more frequent thing from now on.
If Orwell deems the contents of two Datachunks to be contradictory, it will notify you by flagging them as "conflicted."
You will have to make a choice:  which one of the conflicted Datachunks to upload, or to upload neither.
When uploading a conflicted Datachunk, the one you leave behind will be marked as invalid forever. So please, be vigilant!
I see you've found your first non-target person. Good. Orwell will keep track of them, but no Datachunks or new documents for them will appear.
I must authorize them to become a target person first, and I will only do so if I feel it is necessary.
I've seen enough. I believe we have sufficiently proven Bakay to be involved in the attack on Vhart's school.
This has got to be Raban Vhart's motive behind his actions. He is out for revenge.
The fact that Bakay has kept his role in this from us all this time is not doing him any favor... There will be consequences.
And Vhart knows about it. He has already stated he believes Bakay had something to do with it. He must be out for revenge.
Mhm. So, you think you can redo the Aptitude Test just like that? That's either downright naive, or...
Ohhh. No. No! The surveillance cameras...
... How the hell did that happen?! Where did he get those pic--
Goddamn! This... this will have severe repercussions, I fear. We will have some more containment to do.
Even though we are facing a lot of trouble, you have done well. For now, you may complete your work for today.
Click on the door icon in the upper right corner of your screen. I will be seeing you tomorrow.
Agent, you may have noticed by now that for every Datachunk you upload, ten minutes of time pass due to Orwell's extensive search algorithms.
The events around us will unfold whether or not we are making progress on our case. Keep this in mind.
And he said, "Let there be riots," and there were riots.
Mhm. How did this man gain such an immense influence over his followers?

rehabilitation council PSYCHOLOGICAL REPORT - FINAL REPORT Report No. 536/W783 Date October 10, 2012 Issuing facility Bonton Rehab Council Author (psychotherapist) Karen Levine Patient ID PR-34207 Patient name Raban Vhart Patient date of birth August 15, 1972 Prescribed therapy Weekly counseling in 2-hour-sessions I. AUTHOR I am Karen Levine and have been working for Bonton Rehab as a clinical psychologist since February 2010. My specialist field is psychological trauma. Full details of my qualifications, and project history will be provided separately by my employer, the Bonton Rehab Council. II. PATIENT HISTORY The patient is a Pargesian refugee whom I have been treating since February, 2011. The patient was assigned to Outer Bonton Reception Camp, where he was treated for symptoms of PTSD. Traumatic events and severe injury that caused the patient to lose his ability to walk in his home country, coupled with his recent immigration to The Nation are the major contributing factors. III. THERAPY PROGRESSION The patient was initially very unresponsive and reclusive, even apathetic in therapy sessions. Occasionally, the patient would even switch to a rather aggressive tone, when I pushed too much towards him revealing details about his experiences and emotional state. This is probably due to the difficult conditions of living in the refugee encampment for an extensive amount of time, which is very much in conflict with the patients personality valuing strong individuality. Around the summer of 2012, the patient went through a sudden change. He seemed to become more outgoing, happier and willing to talk more, though was still trying to keep conversations superficial. Throughout the sessions, the patient has been opening up more and more, and I feel like we have been making tremendous progress. In addition, the patient also seemed to much more appreciated by fellow camp residents. As of writing this, I am unsure whether to attribute the change to the effects of the therapy or to external factors. IV. THERAPIST'S RECOMMENDATION Although the patient seems to have made a lot of progress during the past months, I strongly recommend to extend therapy and would like to keep him under close observation personally to ensure the progress achieved by the patient so far continues, and to determine the exact cause of the change in personality. I will therefore file a request for extending therapy by six months.

rehabilitation council PSYCHOLOGICAL REPORT - FINAL REPORT Report No. 547/W798 Date October 10, 2012 Issuing facility Bonton Rehab Council Author (psychotherapist) Karen Levine Patient ID PR-28529 Patient name Ilya Vhart Patient date of birth May 18, 1983 Prescribed therapy Weekly counseling in 2-hour-sessions I. AUTHOR I am Karen Levine and have been working for Bonton Rehab as a clinical psychologist since February 2010. My specialist field is psychological trauma. Full details of my qualifications, and project history will be provided separately by my employer, the Bonton Rehab Council. II. PATIENT HISTORY The patient immigrated to The Nation in February 2011 as a Pargesian refugee after his brother was involved in a school bombing, losing his ability to walk. I have known and treated the patient since that time. III. THERAPY PROGRESSION While being very shy and unwilling to talk about details of his experiences for several weeks at the beginning of therapy, the patient has slowly become more outgoing and open after several sessions. His optimism and overall social and friendly character, as well as a tendency to look at the bright side of things has helped him a lot to get through the psychological trauma of the hardships he and his brother faced back in Parges. As the patient slowly opened up, he especially emphasized his 'heroic escape from military service in Parges' at multiple occasions. He also seemed to become more and more interested in myself and my private life, making it difficult for me to maintain my professional distance as a therapist. I also talked to him about his brother and his increasingly reclusive behavior, urging Ilya to assist his disabled brother. IV. THERAPIST'S RECOMMENDATION The therapy is regarded as successful and I am happy to dismiss my patient as largely recovered from his symptoms. However, I feel that it would be very important for him and his brother to leave the refugee camp as soon as possible in order to maintain their mental health.

rehabilitation council PSYCHOLOGICAL REPORT - FINAL REPORT Report No. 536/W783 Date October 10, 2012 Issuing facility Bonton Rehab Council Author (psychotherapist) Karen Levine Patient ID PR-34207 Patient name Raban Vhart Patient date of birth August 15, 1972 Prescribed therapy Weekly counseling in 2-hour-sessions I. AUTHOR I am Karen Levine and have been working for Bonton Rehab as a clinical psychologist since February 2010. My specialist field is psychological trauma. Full details of my qualifications, and project history will be provided separately by my employer, the Bonton Rehab Council. II. PATIENT HISTORY The patient is a Pargesian refugee whom I have been treating since February, 2011. The patient was assigned to Outer Bonton Reception Camp, where he was treated for symptoms of PTSD. Traumatic events and severe injury that caused the patient to lose his ability to walk in his home country, coupled with his recent immigration to The Nation are the major contributing factors. III. THERAPY PROGRESSION The patient was initially very unresponsive and reclusive, even apathetic in therapy sessions. Occasionally, the patient would even switch to a rather aggressive tone, when I pushed too much towards him revealing details about his experiences and emotional state. This is probably due to the difficult conditions of living in the refugee encampment for an extensive amount of time, which is very much in conflict with the patients personality valuing strong individuality. Around the summer of 2012, the patient went through a sudden change. He seemed to become more outgoing, happier and willing to talk more, though was still trying to keep conversations superficial. Throughout the sessions, the patient has been opening up more and more, and I feel like we have been making tremendous progress. In addition, the patient also seemed to much more appreciated by fellow camp residents. As of writing this, I am unsure whether to attribute the change to the effects of the therapy or to external factors. IV. THERAPIST'S RECOMMENDATION Although the patient seems to have made a lot of progress during the past months, I strongly recommend to extend therapy and would like to keep him under close observation personally to ensure the progress achieved by the patient so far continues, and to determine the exact cause of the change in personality. I will therefore file a request for extending therapy by six months.

His optimism and overall social and friendly character, as well as a tendency to look at the bright side of things has helped him a lot to get through the psychological trauma of the hardships he and his brother faced back in Parges. I also talked to him about his brother and his increasingly reclusive behavior, urging Ilya to assist his disabled brother. IV. THERAPIST'S RECOMMENDATION The therapy is regarded as successful and I am happy to dismiss my patient as largely recovered from his symptoms. However, I feel that it would be very important for him and his brother to leave the refugee camp as soon as possible in order to maintain their mental health.
